payload.answer: Open ideas about knowledge graphs include: 1) Knowledge graphs model entities and relationships as nodes and edges, enabling flexible, graph-based queries and reasoning. 2) Core open questions involve measuring semantic richness, scalability to large/real-time data, and designing schemas that tolerate domain evolution. 3) Potential next steps: explore literature on ontology alignment, embedding-based link prediction, storage/compute trade-offs (e.g., graph databases vs. RDF stores), and lightweight prototyping with Python libraries (rdflib, NetworkX, PyKEEN) to validate ideas.

entity_resolution.reason: storing exploratory thoughts about knowledge graphs and potential follow-up action

memory_cards.statement: Knowledge graphs represent entities and relationships as a graph structure, enabling flexible queries, inference, and integration across domains.
Key open questions: how to measure semantic richness, scalability with large data, and effective schema design for evolving domains.

details: Memory entries created for exploratory ideas
